name: Shivam Sharma
about: I’m a Computer Science (Data Science) student who enjoys figuring out how systems actually work behind the scenes. I’m especially interested in the intersection of cybersecurity, data science, and systems, and most of the things I learn usually start from a simple curiosity that turns into a project.

Recently I’ve been building a lightweight network intrusion detection system using Suricata on a Raspberry Pi, trying to make something that could realistically monitor a home network and send alerts through an MQTT pipeline to a dashboard. What started as a small idea ended up pulling me into networking, packet inspection, routing issues, and a lot of trial-and-error debugging. I enjoy that process a lot—breaking things, understanding why they broke, and then rebuilding them properly.

Alongside security work, I’m also exploring data science with Python, mainly using tools like NumPy and Pandas. I like the idea of using data to understand patterns in systems, especially when it comes to security and monitoring.

I also spend time practicing algorithms and problem solving, mostly through competitive programming style problems. I like the challenge of finding cleaner or more efficient solutions, and it’s something I’m trying to get better at consistently.

Outside of tech, I enjoy things that involve strategy and storytelling. I used to play a lot of chess, and I’m getting back into it as a casual hobby. I’m also a big fan of fantasy books, especially the Harry Potter series, and I’m planning to explore more authors like Brandon Sanderson. I find that stories and strategy both influence how I think about building systems—understanding patterns, anticipating outcomes, and thinking a few steps ahead.

In general, I’m someone who learns best by building things and experimenting, even if it means running into a few walls along the way. Right now I’m focused on improving my skills in cybersecurity, backend systems, and data-driven problem solving, while continuing to work on projects that teach me something new every time.

projects.title: Raspberry Pi Network Intrusion Detection System
projects.description: Lightweight network intrusion detection system deployed on Raspberry Pi using Suricata to detect suspicious network activity and analyze packets.

projects.title: Insightlify – WhatsApp Chat Analyzer
projects.description: Web application that analyzes WhatsApp chat exports and generates insights including activity statistics, user interaction patterns, and visual analytics.

labs.title: WebStrike
labs.description: Analyzed network traffic using Wireshark to investigate web server compromise and detect suspicious communication patterns.

labs.title: Oski Malware Analysis

labs.title: Poisoned Credentials
labs.description: Investigated LLMNR/NBT-NS poisoning attacks by analyzing captured network packets to identify rogue machines.

labs.description: Studied remote access trojan artifacts and analyzed malware indicators using threat intelligence platforms.
